an11.15:1.1:
1
"Bhikkhus, when the liberation of the mind by loving-kindness has been pursued, developed, and cultivated, made a vehicle and basis, carried out, consolidated, and properly undertaken, eleven benefits are to be expected.
an11.15:1.3: "One sleeps well; one awakens happily; one does not have bad dreams; one is pleasing to human beings; one is pleasing to spirits; deities protect one; fire, poison, and weapons do not injure one; one's mind quickly becomes concentrated; one's facial complexion is serene; one dies unconfused; and if one does not penetrate further, one fares on to the brahmā world.
an11.15:1.4: "When, bhikkhus, the liberation of the mind by loving-kindness has been repeatedly pursued, developed, and cultivated, made a vehicle and basis, carried out, consolidated, and properly undertaken, these eleven benefits are to be expected."
